Live QRISK3

Explore estimated 10-year cardiovascular risk using the QRISK3 variables. This is a dynamic tool: the score updates live as you adjust any parameter, making it easy to see how risk changes with age, blood pressure, smoking status, and other clinical factors.

QRISK3 is validated for ages 25 to 84.
This is the standard deviation of repeated systolic blood pressure measurements. If you only have a single reading or no variability estimate, leave this as 0.
Townsend score is a measure of material deprivation. Higher values indicate greater deprivation. If you do not have a Townsend value for the person, use 0 as a neutral baseline.
Use 0 if you do not have a Townsend deprivation value.

Clinical Flags

QRISK3 defines severe mental illness as psychosis, schizophrenia, or bipolar affective disease.
This means a first-degree relative with angina or heart attack before age 60.
Ready
--
Enter the patient details and calculate the score.

Technical Payload Preview

This payload is the exact JavaScript data object the calculator uses internally. It is shown so you can see the values that would be sent to an API later if this tool is connected to a backend. 

{}

This implementation should be checked against the original QRISK3 source and qrisk.org before patient-facing use. Inaccurate implementations can lead to wrong patients being given the wrong treatment.


About QRISK3

QRISK3 estimates 10-year cardiovascular risk using demographic, biometric, and clinical inputs. It is intended for adults without established cardiovascular disease and is commonly used to support prevention discussions alongside clinical judgement.

Privacy

This page does not store submitted values.

Reference

Hippisley-Cox J, Coupland C, Brindle P. Development and validation of QRISK3 risk prediction algorithms to estimate future risk of cardiovascular disease. BMJ. 2017;357:j2099.
